comment: Microloom — a Microcosm-style granular pitch-shimmer cloud delay. Records audio into a circular buffer, sprays pitch-shifted grains, regenerates them with feedback, and washes the result through a reverb tail. Desktop preview implements the full DSP; the pedal C port is a staged effort (see README).
gid_comment: 0x08 = Delay category. Granular delay is the core mechanism.
fxid_comment: 0x01C2 = above the repo's used range (400-444). Verify uniqueness before flashing.

params.name: Pitch
params.comment: Grain pitch in semitones, -12..+12. 50=unison, 100=+1 octave (classic shimmer).

params.name: Dense
params.comment: Grain overlap/voice count and cloud thickness.

params.name: Spray
params.comment: Random scatter of grain read positions for the diffuse texture.

params.name: Regen
params.comment: Feedback: grains regenerate into the buffer, building the cloud and the rising shimmer cascade.

params.name: Verb
params.comment: Reverb wash amount applied to the granular output.

audio_nop_comment: Pedal C port starts as a UI-only smoke build. Full granular DSP is validated on desktop first; see tools/audio_preview/renderers/microloom.py.
